title: Gratuitous .clone() on cli.rs data_dir
description:
src/cli.rs:189 — Inside Command::Post handler:

    let data_dir = self
        .data_dir
        .as_ref()
        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("..."))?
        .clone();

The .clone() creates an owned PathBuf, but data_dir is only used by reference thereafter (passed to broadside::media::process_local as &data_dir). The clone is unnecessary — a &PathBuf (from as_ref()) would suffice.

Impact: Unnecessary allocation. Per standards: 'Don't .clone() to escape the borrow checker. Clone is for when independent ownership is genuinely needed.'
